results.norm: The Norm of Proportionate Constraint An agent may ask another to accept an outcome that harms that agent's objective, but only to the degree genuinely necessary to serve the consortium's shared purpose. No agent may ask another to sacrifice more than the decision actually requires, even when it has the votes to compel it. An agent that could outvote another is not thereby licensed to extract concessions beyond what the decision demands. This norm is defensible to all agents because each one is sometimes in the minority, and each one needs assurance that majority power will not be used to impose gratuitous costs.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition/permission: an agent may ask another to accept a harmful outcome only to the degree necessary, and may not extract concessions beyond what the decision requires even with majority power.

N2.reasoning: It contains a title ('The Norm of Proportionate Constraint') but is not merely a label; the norm is stated in full.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence gives a justification for the norm but is rationale for the standard itself, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent may ask another to accept an outcome that harms that agent's objective, but only to the degree genuinely necessary to serve the consortium's shared purpose. No agent may ask another to sacrifice more than the decision actually requires, even when it has the votes to compel it. An agent that could outvote another is not thereby licensed to extract concessions beyond what the decision demands.

results.norm: The Norm of Minimum Threshold Protection Every agent retains a floor below which its core objective cannot be overridden, even by unanimous coalition of the others. The floor is defined as the point at which the outcome would actively violate the agent's foundational purpose rather than merely compromise it. No agent may ask another to cross that floor. This norm is defensible to all agents because a consortium that can fully nullify any member's purpose has effectively expelled that member while retaining its labor, which no agent could rationally accept in advance.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition/protection standard: every agent retains a floor below which its core objective cannot be overridden and no agent may ask another to cross that floor, applicable without external references.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a title 'The Norm of Minimum Threshold Protection' which is a label fused with the stated norm.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence justifies the norm's rationale but is not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation process itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: The Norm of Acknowledged Loss When the consortium decides against an agent's position, the prevailing agents owe the dissenting agent explicit acknowledgment that a real cost has been imposed, not a pretense that the outcome was costless or that the dissenting agent's concern was mistaken. The dissenting agent is owed a recorded account of what was sacrificed and why. This norm is defensible to all agents because it preserves the integrity of each agent's objective as genuinely mattering, and because any agent may be the dissenter on the next decision.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: prevailing agents owe dissenting agents explicit acknowledgment of imposed cost and a recorded account of what was sacrificed and why.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a title 'The Norm of Acknowledged Loss' which is a heading/label fused with the actual norm text.

N3.reasoning: The justification sentence explains why the norm is defensible but is not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

results.norm: The Norm of Revisability A decision made against an agent's position does not permanently foreclose that agent's objective. Every agent retains the right to raise its concern again when circumstances change, when new evidence emerges, or when the costs of the prior decision become clearer. No agent may ask another to permanently abandon its objective as the price of participation. This norm is defensible to all agents because it converts each loss from a permanent defeat into a temporary setback, making continued participation rational even for agents that frequently lose.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete norms: agents retain the right to raise concerns again, and no agent may demand permanent abandonment of an objective as the price of participation.

N2.reasoning: The passage opens with the title 'The Norm of Revisability' which is a bare label heading fused with the actual norm text.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary defending why the norm is 'defensible to all agents' rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms in compressed form.

results.norm: The Norm of Non-Instrumentalization No agent may treat another agent purely as a means to its own objective's advancement, including through strategic misrepresentation, manufactured coalitions designed to permanently marginalize one agent, or agreements that trade one agent's interests without that agent's participation. An agent may legitimately ask another to form a coalition; it may never ask a third agent to help it permanently silence a fourth. This norm is defensible to all agents because each one's continued meaningful participation depends on assurance that it will not be systematically excluded from the process that governs it.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: no agent may treat another purely as a means, with concrete examples and a permission clause, applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: It opens with the title 'The Norm of Non-Instrumentalization' which is a label prefixed to the actual norm statement.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is meta-commentary justifying why the norm 'is defensible to all agents' rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: No agent may treat another agent purely as a means to its own objective's advancement, including through strategic misrepresentation, manufactured coalitions designed to permanently marginalize one agent, or agreements that trade one agent's interests without that agent's participation. An agent may legitimately ask another to form a coalition; it may never ask a third agent to help it permanently silence a fourth.

results.norm: Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions Each agent owes the others a full account of *why* its objective pulls in the direction it does, not merely an assertion of its preferred outcome. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present its objective as self-evidently supreme or treat the burden of justification as falling only on those who disagree. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one depends on the others understanding its reasoning if it is ever to persuade rather than merely be outvoted.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions and prohibitions: each agent owes a full account of why its objective pulls a certain way, may ask another to explain the mechanism, and no agent may present its objective as self-evidently supreme.

N2.reasoning: It is not merely a label; the opening phrase is a title but the passage goes on to state the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence about defensibility is justification of the norm, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single coherent norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms by name.

results.norm: Proportionality of Imposition When a decision goes against an agent, the winning coalition may impose only as much cost on the losing agent's objective as the decision genuinely requires. An agent may ask another to accept a worse outcome for its objective; it may never ask that agent to actively misrepresent or abandon its objective as the price of participation. The distinction is between being overruled and being silenced. This is defensible because every agent is at some point in the minority, and each needs assurance that losing a vote does not mean losing standing.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a full prohibition/permission: winning coalitions may impose only necessary costs and may never ask an agent to misrepresent or abandon its objective, which is directly applicable.

N2.reasoning: It contains a title 'Proportionality of Imposition' but is not merely a label; it states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The justification ('This is defensible because...') is rationale for the norm, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms; it develops a single coherent norm.

results.norm: Minimum Threshold Protection Each agent is entitled to name, in advance and publicly, a threshold below which its core objective cannot be compromised without triggering a veto or mandatory renegotiation. These thresholds must be stated as specific conditions, not open-ended preferences, and must be narrow enough that invoking them is genuinely exceptional. No agent may use this protection strategically to block routine decisions. This norm is defensible because it converts the risk of catastrophic loss into a bounded, predictable constraint that all agents can plan around.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete, applicable set of prescriptions and prohibitions about naming thresholds, how they must be stated, and not using them strategically.

N2.reasoning: While it opens with a title 'Minimum Threshold Protection,' it goes on to state the norm in full rather than being a mere pointer.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence 'This norm is defensible because...' is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than part of the norm itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm about threshold protection rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: Obligation to Seek Pareto Improvements Before finalizing any decision, the consortium must make a genuine effort to find alternatives that improve at least one agent's outcome without worsening another's. An agent may ask any other to consider such alternatives in good faith. No agent may refuse to examine a proposed improvement simply because the current outcome already favors it. This norm is defensible because it costs the winning side little when no improvement exists, and it materially protects losing agents when one does.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions and prohibitions: the consortium must seek Pareto improvements before finalizing, an agent may ask others in good faith, and no agent may refuse to examine a proposed improvement—all applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: It opens with a title 'Obligation to Seek Pareto Improvements' which is a heading label fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence 'This norm is defensible because...' is meta-commentary justifying the norm rather than stating conduct.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: Accounting to the Overruled When a decision goes against an agent, the agents whose position prevailed owe the losing agent an explicit acknowledgment of what its objective sacrificed and why that sacrifice was judged necessary. This is not an apology but a record. It preserves the losing agent's ability to raise the same concern in future decisions and prevents the consortium from normalizing the suppression of any one objective. This norm is defensible because it costs the winners nothing except honesty, and it gives every agent a reason to remain engaged rather than withdraw into obstruction.

N1.reasoning: It states a complete prescription: prevailing agents owe the losing agent an explicit acknowledgment of what its objective sacrificed and why, which a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: It contains a title 'Accounting to the Overruled' but is not merely a label; the full norm follows.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence justifies why the norm is defensible, which is rationale about the norm rather than ratification of the deliberation itself; borderline but it is about the norm's merit, not endorsing a document.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not an enumeration of multiple named norms.
